codeReview.summary: F018 (Add manual sync button functionality) is fully implemented as part of the F017 SyncStatusBar component. All required behaviors are present: the button triggers immediate sync, is hidden during active sync (functionally disabled), shows progress via spinner and percentage, resets the auto-sync timer, and displays success/error feedback via toast notifications. The code passes typecheck and lint with no errors.

issues.description: Manual sync only processes the first configured module (timerState.config.modules[0]). If multiple modules have pending changes, users must wait for the next auto-sync cycle or trigger manual sync again. Consider batching all configured modules in a single manual sync call.

The manual sync button is completely hidden (conditional rendering) rather than visibly disabled during sync. While this functionally prevents clicks, a disabled visible button with a spinner would provide clearer UX feedback that a sync is in progress.

The isSyncing guard in triggerManualSync() is a simple boolean check without atomic locking. In a single-threaded Electron main process this is practically safe, but a more robust guard could reject concurrent IPC invocations at the handler level.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No project conventions were violated. The worker followed the existing IPC pattern (preload → renderer context → main process handler → autoSyncTimer) consistently.

The worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure. The transcript shows the worker first gathered context, then inspected existing SyncStatusBar and SyncContext code, verified the manual sync flow end-to-end, and ran baseline validators before completing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows verification commands: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run test, git status — all passing. No skill deviations noted.

summary: Feature F018 passes review. The manual sync button functionality is correctly wired through the full stack (renderer → preload → main → autoSyncTimer) and satisfies all validation contract assertions it fulfills: MANUAL_SYNC_TRIGGER, BUTTON_DISABLED_DURING_SYNC, and FEEDBACK_DISPLAYED. All identified issues are minor UX or enhancement opportunities, not blocking defects.
